title: 6LACK

infobox.value: Ricardo Valdez Valentine Jr.[[1]](#cite_note-1)   (1992-06-24) June 24, 1992 (age 33)  [Baltimore](/wiki/Baltimore "Baltimore"), Maryland, U.S.

(born June 24, 1992), known professionally as **6lack** (stylized as **6LACK**, pronounced "black"),[[4]](#cite_note-rs-4) is an American [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") singer and songwriter. Born in [Baltimore](/wiki/Baltimore "Baltimore"), Maryland, and raised in [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta"), Georgia, he gained recognition following the release of his 2016 single "[Prblms](/wiki/Prblms "Prblms")", which marked his first entry on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"). He signed with [Love Renaissance](/wiki/Love_Renaissance "Love Renaissance"), an imprint of Interscope Records to release his debut studio album, *[Free 6lack](/wiki/Free_6lack "Free 6lack")* (2016), which was nominated for [Best Urban Contemporary Album](/wiki/Best_Urban_Contemporary_Album "Best Urban Contemporary Album") at the [60th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/60th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "60th Annual Grammy Awards") and received [platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") certification by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [6LACK](/w/index.php?title=6LACK&redirect=no "6LACK"))

American singer

Musical artist

**Ricardo Valdez Valentine Jr.** (born June 24, 1992), known professionally as **6lack** (stylized as **6LACK**, pronounced "black"),[[4]](#cite_note-rs-4) is an American [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") singer and songwriter. Born in [Baltimore](/wiki/Baltimore "Baltimore"), Maryland, and raised in [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta"), Georgia, he gained recognition following the release of his 2016 single "[Prblms](/wiki/Prblms "Prblms")", which marked his first entry on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"). He signed with [Love Renaissance](/wiki/Love_Renaissance "Love Renaissance"), an imprint of Interscope Records to release his debut studio album, *[Free 6lack](/wiki/Free_6lack "Free 6lack")* (2016), which was nominated for [Best Urban Contemporary Album](/wiki/Best_Urban_Contemporary_Album "Best Urban Contemporary Album") at the [60th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/60th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "60th Annual Grammy Awards") and received [platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") certification by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Released two years later, his second studio album, *[East Atlanta Love Letter](/wiki/East_Atlanta_Love_Letter "East Atlanta Love Letter")* (2018), peaked at number three on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), while his single "[OTW](/wiki/OTW_(Khalid,_Ty_Dolla_Sign_and_6lack_song) "OTW (Khalid, Ty Dolla Sign and 6lack song)")" (with [Khalid](/wiki/Khalid_(American_singer) "Khalid (American singer)") and [Ty Dolla Sign](/wiki/Ty_Dolla_Sign "Ty Dolla Sign")), received [double platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") certification by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA). His debut extended play (EP) *[6pc Hot](/wiki/6pc_Hot_EP "6pc Hot EP")* (2020), included the single "[Know My Rights](/wiki/Know_My_Rights "Know My Rights")" (featuring [Lil Baby](/wiki/Lil_Baby "Lil Baby")). Later that year, he guest performed alongside [Elton John](/wiki/Elton_John "Elton John") on [Gorillaz](/wiki/Gorillaz "Gorillaz")'s single "[The Pink Phantom](/wiki/The_Pink_Phantom "The Pink Phantom")". His 2021 single, "[Calling My Phone](/wiki/Calling_My_Phone "Calling My Phone")" (featuring [Lil Tjay](/wiki/Lil_Tjay "Lil Tjay")), peaked at number three on the *Billboard* Hot 100 and peaked atop the [Canadian Hot 100](/wiki/Canadian_Hot_100 "Canadian Hot 100"); it remains his highest-charting song on the former chart.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) His third album, *[Since I Have a Lover](/wiki/Since_I_Have_a_Lover "Since I Have a Lover")* (2023), was met with moderate critical and commercial reception.

6lack joined the Atlanta-Baltimore musical collective [Spillage Village](/wiki/Spillage_Village "Spillage Village")—founded by [EarthGang](/wiki/EarthGang "EarthGang") and [JID](/wiki/JID "JID")—in 2014. He has received three [Grammy Award](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") nominations.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=6lack&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Ricardo Valdez Valentine Jr. was born on June 24, 1992, in Baltimore, Maryland, and moved to [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta"), [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(U.S._state) "Georgia (U.S. state)"), with his parents in 1997.[[9]](#cite_note-9) He attended [Stone Mountain High School](/wiki/Stone_Mountain_High_School "Stone Mountain High School") and [Newton High School](/wiki/Newton_High_School_(Georgia) "Newton High School (Georgia)").[[10]](#cite_note-10) He is the eldest of three siblings. Valentine's first [recording](/wiki/Sound_recording_and_reproduction "Sound recording and reproduction") experience was at 4 years of age, at his father's studio.[[11]](#cite_note-11) He began rapping in middle school as a [battle rapper](/wiki/Battle_rap "Battle rap") and was involved in many freestyle battles in his youth including against rapper [Young Thug](/wiki/Young_Thug "Young Thug").[[12]](#cite_note-:0-12)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=6lack&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### 2011–2017: Beginnings and *Free 6lack*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=6lack&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2011–2017: Beginnings and Free 6lack")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/24/6LACK.jpg/250px-6LACK.jpg)](/wiki/File:6LACK.jpg)

6lack in January 2017

6lack signed a [record deal](/wiki/Recording_contract "Recording contract") with [Flo Rida](/wiki/Flo_Rida "Flo Rida")'s International Music Group and Strong Arm Records in July 2011. He left [Valdosta State University](/wiki/Valdosta_State_University "Valdosta State University") once he signed, and spent the next few years learning about the industry. 6lack spent five years with the label, putting music out on his [SoundCloud](/wiki/SoundCloud "SoundCloud") account. 6lack had little financial security and spent most of his time sleeping in the studio or outside on the street after moving to [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami"), [Florida](/wiki/Florida "Florida") to work on music with the record label.[[12]](#cite_note-:0-12) 6lack eventually left his label due to issues over artistic and creative license and management.[[13]](#cite_note-auto-13) In 2015, 6lack announced that he had joined the musical collective [Spillage Village](/wiki/Spillage_Village "Spillage Village") after living with the members of [EarthGang](/wiki/EarthGang "EarthGang"), and appeared on four tracks of Spillage Village EP called *Bears Like This Too* in 2015.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Once he left the label, he signed with [Love Renaissance](/wiki/Love_Renaissance "Love Renaissance") and [Interscope Records](/wiki/Interscope_Records "Interscope Records").[[15]](#cite_note-Genius-15) In November 2016, *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* included 6lack in their list of "10 New Artists You Need to Know".[[4]](#cite_note-rs-4) He then went on to release his debut studio album, *[Free 6lack](/wiki/Free_6lack "Free 6lack")*, which peaked at number 34 on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") chart.[[16]](#cite_note-16) The album's single "[Prblms](/wiki/Prblms "Prblms")" peaked at number 73 on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") chart and became his first platinum record.[[17]](#cite_note-17) In April 2017, he joined Canadian singer [The Weeknd](/wiki/The_Weeknd "The Weeknd") as opening act for his [Legend of the Fall](/wiki/Starboy:_Legend_of_the_Fall_Tour "Starboy: Legend of the Fall Tour") tour in North America.

### 2018–present: *East Atlanta Love Letter*, *6pc Hot, and Since I Have a Lover*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=6lack&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2018–present: East Atlanta Love Letter, 6pc Hot, and Since I Have a Lover")]

In April 2018, he released the single "[OTW](/wiki/OTW_(Khalid,_Ty_Dolla_Sign_and_6lack_song) "OTW (Khalid, Ty Dolla Sign and 6lack song)")" with [Khalid](/wiki/Khalid_(American_singer) "Khalid (American singer)") and [Ty Dolla Sign](/wiki/Ty_Dolla_Sign "Ty Dolla Sign"), which has reached at number 57 on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[18]](#cite_note-:1-18) "OTW" became 6lack's highest peaking song on the Hot 100.[[18]](#cite_note-:1-18) The first [single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") for his sophomore album, "Switch", was released on June 22, 2018, with a music video released on July 16.[[19]](#cite_note-19) "Nonchalant" was released as the album's second single on August 17, with its accompanying music video.[[20]](#cite_note-NonchalantHypebeast-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21) On September 14, 2018, 6lack released his second studio album, *[East Atlanta Love Letter](/wiki/East_Atlanta_Love_Letter "East Atlanta Love Letter")*, featuring [guest appearances](/wiki/Guest_appearance "Guest appearance") from [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)"), [J. Cole](/wiki/J._Cole "J. Cole"), [Offset](/wiki/Offset_(rapper) "Offset (rapper)"), and Khalid. The album's release was preceded by the singles "Switch" and "Nonchalant."[[22]](#cite_note-22) In October 2018, 6lack started a tour for the promotion of the album, with opening acts [THEY.](/wiki/THEY. "THEY."), [Tierra Whack](/wiki/Tierra_Whack "Tierra Whack"), [Boogie](/wiki/Westside_Boogie "Westside Boogie"), [Deante' Hitchcock](/wiki/Deante%27_Hitchcock "Deante' Hitchcock"), [Summer Walker](/wiki/Summer_Walker "Summer Walker"), and [Ari Lennox](/wiki/Ari_Lennox "Ari Lennox").[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24) 6lack later featured on several other songs, such as "[Waves](/wiki/Waves_(Normani_song) "Waves (Normani song)")" by [Normani](/wiki/Normani "Normani").[[25]](#cite_note-25) and "Crowded Room" by [Selena Gomez](/wiki/Selena_Gomez "Selena Gomez"), in 2019 and early 2020, respectively.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

In May 2020, 6lack released the track "ATL Freestyle". A few weeks later, on June 24, 2020, 6lack celebrated his birthday with the release of the single "Float". He also announced his EP *[6pc Hot](/wiki/6pc_Hot_EP "6pc Hot EP")*, which was released on June 26 and included the [Lil Baby](/wiki/Lil_Baby "Lil Baby")-assisted single, "[Know My Rights](/wiki/Know_My_Rights "Know My Rights")". He also launched his own hot sauce brand 600 Degrees Original.[[27]](#cite_note-27)
6lack appeared on two tracks from [Spillage Village](/wiki/Spillage_Village "Spillage Village")'s *[Spilligion](/wiki/Spilligion "Spilligion")* album, released in September 2020.[[28]](#cite_note-28) During 2020, he collaborated with British virtual band [Gorillaz](/wiki/Gorillaz "Gorillaz") and singer-songwriter [Elton John](/wiki/Elton_John "Elton John") on the track "[The Pink Phantom](/wiki/The_Pink_Phantom "The Pink Phantom")", for the band's seventh studio album, *[Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez](/wiki/Song_Machine,_Season_One:_Strange_Timez "Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez")*,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] and with rappers [Lil Durk](/wiki/Lil_Durk "Lil Durk") and Young Thug for the song "[Stay Down](/wiki/Stay_Down_(Lil_Durk,_6lack_and_Young_Thug_song) "Stay Down (Lil Durk, 6lack and Young Thug song)")" from the former's sixth studio album, *[The Voice](/wiki/The_Voice_(Lil_Durk_album) "The Voice (Lil Durk album)")*. The song peaked at number 74 on the Billboard Hot 100.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

In 2021, he released "[Calling My Phone](/wiki/Calling_My_Phone "Calling My Phone")" with Bronx rapper [Lil Tjay](/wiki/Lil_Tjay "Lil Tjay").[[30]](#cite_note-30) In 2022, he was featured on Canadian singer-songwriter [Jessie Reyez](/wiki/Jessie_Reyez "Jessie Reyez")'s album [Yessie](/wiki/Yessie "Yessie"), in her song "Forever", and its accompanying music video.[[31]](#cite_note-31)

On March 24, 2023, 6lack released his album *[Since I Have a Lover](/wiki/Since_I_Have_a_Lover "Since I Have a Lover")*. The 19-track album featured the artists [QUIN](/wiki/QUIN_(singer) "QUIN (singer)"), [Wale](/wiki/Wale_(rapper) "Wale (rapper)"), and [Don Toliver](/wiki/Don_Toliver "Don Toliver"). The album was nominated for [best progressive R&B album](/wiki/Best_Progressive_R%26B_Album "Best Progressive R&B Album") at the [Grammys](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"). In an interview with [Complex](/wiki/Complex_Networks "Complex Networks"), 6lack stated that the title responds to questions such as "Where have you been? What have you been doing? What's the inspiration?" that people asked him.[[32]](#cite_note-32) On March 22, 2024, 6lack released his acoustic project *No More Lonely Nights*. It featured 6 songs off his album *Since I Have a Lover*.

## Artistry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=6lack&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Artistry")]

6lack's music focuses or is based on personal and business relationships frequently, especially the topic of [heartbreak](/wiki/Broken_heart "Broken heart").[[33]](#cite_note-33) 6lack has said that his songwriting is influenced mainly by his personal experiences and failed relationships.[[34]](#cite_note-34) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* magazine has described 6lack's music as "moody hip hop" that "spotlights vulnerability and honesty in a way the emoji generation can understand."[[13]](#cite_note-auto-13) 6lack has cited [Sade](/wiki/Sade_(singer) "Sade (singer)"), [T-Pain](/wiki/T-Pain "T-Pain"), [The-Dream](/wiki/The-Dream "The-Dream"), and [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(musician) "Usher (musician)") as his influences.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=6lack&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Personal life")]

6lack has two daughters, born in 2017 and 2025.[[36]](#cite_note-36)[[37]](#cite_note-37)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=6lack&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Discography")]

Main article: [6lack discography](/wiki/6lack_discography "6lack discography")

* *[Free 6lack](/wiki/Free_6lack "Free 6lack")* (2016)
* *[East Atlanta Love Letter](/wiki/East_Atlanta_Love_Letter "East Atlanta Love Letter")* (2018)
* *[Since I Have a Lover](/wiki/Since_I_Have_a_Lover "Since I Have a Lover")* (2023)
* *Love is the New Gangsta* (2026)

## Tours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=6lack&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Tours")]

**Headlining**

* Free 6lack Tour (2017)
* From East Atlanta with Love Tour (2018)
* Since I Have a Lover Tour (2023)

**Supporting**

* [Starboy: Legend of the Fall Tour](/wiki/Starboy:_Legend_of_the_Fall_Tour "Starboy: Legend of the Fall Tour") (with [The Weeknd](/wiki/The_Weeknd "The Weeknd")) (2017)
* [Heartbreak on a Full Moon Tour](/wiki/Heartbreak_on_a_Full_Moon#Tour "Heartbreak on a Full Moon") (with [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown")) (2018)
* It Was You All Along Tour (with [Russ](/wiki/Russ_(rapper) "Russ (rapper)")) (2024)
